title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. pumila
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. pumila, also known as dwarf bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of arbutin, a compound that has antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of dwarf bearberry have been found to provide various health benefits, including relief from urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Dwarf bearberry can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health enthusiasts.
